evals.query: Write a JUnit 5 component test for my Kora UserService. It has a method createUser(UserRequest) that returns a UserResponse. Inject the real service from the application graph.
expected_behavior: Annotates the test class with @KoraAppTest(Application.class) from ru.tinkoff.kora.test.extension.junit5
Injects UserService with @TestComponent
Adds testImplementation "ru.tinkoff.kora:test-junit5" and the mandatory annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors"
Does not use foreign-framework test annotations or field injection

evals.query: I need to test OrderService which depends on UserRepository, but I want to mock the repository inside the Kora graph and verify the calls.
expected_behavior: Combines @Mock with @TestComponent on the UserRepository field so the mock enters the Kora graph
Stubs with Mockito.when(...).thenReturn(...) and checks interactions with verify(...)
Explicitly avoids @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) because it conflicts with @KoraAppTest
Does not rely on a mock-bean construct or any foreign-framework testing construct

evals.query: How do I override configuration in a Kora test, substituting environment-style placeholders like POSTGRES_JDBC_URL without a separate config file?
expected_behavior: Implements KoraAppTestConfigModifier and returns a KoraConfigModification from config()
Uses KoraConfigModification.ofSystemProperty(...).withSystemProperty(...) (or ofString/ofResourceFile)
Notes that KoraAppTestConfigModifier must be used via the interface method, not the constructor
Does not use a foreign @TestPropertySource mechanism or application-test.properties conventions

evals.query: Write an integration test for my UserRepository against a real PostgreSQL using Testcontainers, with Flyway migrations applied.
expected_behavior: Uses @Testcontainers with a static @Container PostgreSQLContainer
Implements KoraAppTestConfigModifier to feed POSTGRES.getJdbcUrl()/getUsername()/getPassword() into the db config
Adds ru.tinkoff.kora:database-jdbc and ru.tinkoff.kora:database-flyway plus the testcontainers postgresql artifact, and sets flyway.locations
Does not use a foreign data/ORM layer, a JdbcTemplate, or a @DataJpaTest slice

evals.query: My production graph has no cleanup repository. How do I add a test-only repository with deleteAll() and findAll() for integration tests?
expected_behavior: Declares a @KoraApp TestApplication interface extending the production Application in the test source set
Adds an inner @Repository interface extending JdbcRepository with @Query methods, plus a @Root @Tag default method that consumes it so it enters the graph

Enables -Akora.app.submodule.enabled=true on the production module and adds testAnnotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors", excluding generated classes via filter excludeTestsMatching '*$*'
Does not use a foreign-framework @Repository bean or classpath component scanning

evals.query: In a Kora test I want to add a Supplier<Integer> component to the graph and also replace an existing component with a value derived from the real one.
expected_behavior: Implements KoraAppTestGraphModifier and returns KoraGraphModification.create()
Uses addComponent(TypeRef.of(Supplier.class, Integer.class), ...) and replaceComponent(...) with a graph -> graph.getFirst(TypeRef.of(...)) lambda
Passes the @Tag class list as the middle argument of replaceComponent when the target is tagged
Does not use foreign @Primary/@Qualifier annotations or ApplicationContext bean overriding

evals.query: I want a partial mock (spy) of a Kora component and I want unused stubs to fail the test.
expected_behavior: Uses @Spy with @TestComponent, optionally from a field initializer, to keep original behavior
Adds @MockitoStrictness(Strictness.STRICT_STUBS) so unused stubs raise UnnecessaryStubbingException
Keeps the Mockito lifecycle under @KoraAppTest without attaching MockitoExtension
Does not introduce foreign-framework test slices or PowerMock

evals.query: Write an integration test for a Kora @KafkaListener and @KafkaPublisher against a Kafka container.
expected_behavior: Uses @Testcontainers with a static @Container KafkaContainer and a long withStartupTimeout
Feeds KAFKA.getBootstrapServers() into per-listener driverProperties { "bootstrap.servers" = ... } with offset = "earliest", not a flat kafka.bootstrapServers key
Verifies asynchronous delivery with Awaitility instead of Thread.sleep
Does not use a foreign Kafka test framework, @EmbeddedKafka, or a KafkaTemplate
